findings.description: The PDF uses a two-column layout for Receipts and Disbursements, with FY 2020-2021 data on the left and Duration of Program data on the right. The HTML converts this into a single four-column table, which changes the visual layout from side-by-side independent sections to a merged tabular structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two distinct column groups (FY 2020-2021 on left, Duration of Program on right) with separate headers. HTML merges them into one table with four columns.

findings.description: In the PDF, the Receipts and Disbursements sections are visually separated with whitespace and distinct section headers with underlines. In the HTML, they are combined into a single continuous table, making the section breaks less distinct.
findings.evidence: PDF has clear visual separation between Receipts section and Disbursements section with blank rows and underlined headers. HTML table runs them together with 'Receipts:' and 'Disbursements:' as regular rows.

findings.description: The Fund Balance and Encumbered in Contracts summary in the PDF appears as standalone right-aligned text, while in the HTML it is rendered as a separate two-column table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Fund Balance as of 3/31/2021: $ 13,054,308.66' as formatted text. HTML renders it as a Description/Amount table.

findings.description: The DSCA Site Statistics in the PDF is presented as a simple label-value list format. The HTML converts it into a formal Statistic/Value table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows statistics as indented text with values aligned, HTML shows a structured table with headers 'Statistic' and 'Value'.

findings.description: HTML adds column headers ('Description', 'Amount', 'Statistic', 'Value', 'FY 2020-2021 (through 3/31/21) Category', 'FY 2020-2021 (through 3/31/21) Amount', 'Duration of Program (through 3/31/21) Category', 'Duration of Program (through 3/31/21) Amount') that do not explicitly appear in the PDF. These are reasonable structural additions for table accessibility but are technically not in the source.
findings.evidence: PDF has no explicit column header rows for its tables. HTML adds <th> headers like 'Statistic', 'Value', 'Description', 'Amount', etc.

findings.description: The main financial table in the HTML merges FY 2020-2021 and Duration of Program data into a single 4-column table, which duplicates the category labels (e.g., 'Receipts:', 'Solvent Tax Revenue:') in both columns 1 and 3. This is an acceptable structural choice, but the original PDF presents them as two side-by-side sections with non-redundant labels.
findings.evidence: Each row has the category name repeated in column 1 and column 3, e.g., 'Solvent Tax Revenue:' appears twice per row.

findings.description: The HTML has two nested <main> elements, which is a structural duplication in the markup, though it does not cause content duplication visible to users.
findings.evidence: HTML source contains <main> nested inside another <main>.
